All right, what is the weekly marriage super set? Okay, I made up the name super set. I saw it sound cool because we liked the lid. Right, it is basically a one-on-one with your spouse if you work together. So this is for those of you who are working and running the business with your spouse or your significant other. It's basically a time to discuss your priorities for the week ahead, as well as review how you did last week relationship-wise, okay? So it's kind of a combination of a two-in-one with business and romance. So there's two parts to this. And here's an example of how we use this. One, you wanna decide where you're going to meet, what day and time, put it in your calendar, it doesn't move. Okay? And I do suggest that this be the first meeting you have of the week, meaning you meet before you have a team meeting, you meet before you have one-on-one, you meet before a huddle, anything. Okay, so it's like the first meeting on Monday morning. You wanna set the agenda. So typically it looks like we will discuss our priorities for the week ahead, what's coming up, are we traveling, are there events, what's on the calendar we both need to be aware of, do we have things planned with friends, who's planning it, et cetera. What is not on the calendar that should be, do you have a date night in, do you have time with your family or spouse put in? You know, make sure to schedule this and then include location, activity, reach out to any, you know, restaurants or activity vendors or whatever if you need to, okay? Four is sharing your to-do list with each other and reviewing each other's priorities. So you've got a to-do list for the week, exchanging it with your spouse or partner and making sure that you don't have any overlapping that you're not doing any double work, right? Five is sharing and asking where they need support. So say Alex is redoing all of legacy videos and then I look at his plate and I'm like, wow, I'm like, hey, you know why, Alex? Can I help you? Do you need support? Maybe I could do these sections. You know, I helped build these parts out. You're doing, you did the videos for them, but like we could tag team it and I could do this. And then you might be like, yeah, of course, which of course you did. And then lastly, you assign deadlines to each of you, right, if applicable. So saying like, hey, I'll make sure I get this done by Friday or Alex might come to me and say, hey, we really need to do this. I'm like, all right, cool. I'm gonna communicate to the team day, make sure it gets done no later than next month. Right? Part two is where we review our marriage. So basically it's a reflection for each of you. And the reason that we do this is not because it's honestly just keeping it in the forefront of your mind. So if you're putting time to your marriage, your marriage will put time back into you. I don't know how else better to say it. And maybe I could say attention. If you put attention on your marriage, it will give you attention back. Okay. So one, rating each other. How am I doing on a scale of one to 10? Okay. One being I suck and you don't wanna be married to me. I'm just kidding. But like one being like, you felt like I was totally gone last week and like we had no interaction at all. 10 being I'm doing such a great job. You can't think of anything better, right? Question number two, did we have enough quality sex this week? I'm serious when I talk about this. Alex and I discussed that. You know, how's our sex life? Do you feel it's good? Do you feel like we are making improvements there? Do you feel like we're trying new things? Do you feel like we're stuck in a rut? Like how do we feel about it, right? And the thing is you don't wanna talk about sex in the bedroom. You wanna talk about sex outside the bedroom. Okay. You wanna talk about it logically because we can all talk about sex logically. We can also all talk about sex emotionally. You do not wanna do this in emotional state. You wanna talk about it logically because logic solves problems. Three, did we spend enough quality time together? Do you feel like you got to really be with that person the past week, right? Poor, do you feel loved and supported? Do you feel like how they showed up for you last week was in a loving, caring, supportive way? Or do you maybe there were situations that arose that you felt like they could have supported you better? And then lastly is one thing I can do to raise my score. So I'll look at Alex and be like, how can I be a better wife to you this week? You know, he'd be like, you know, how can I be a world-class husband for you? And then we just tell each other, like here's one thing you could do better, right? And most of the time we each already know what we could do better, but it is good to remind each other and hold each other accountable to the things that we're working on, okay? And so I'm telling you, if you right now don't feel like you're on the same page as your partner and you don't do this, then you're basically asking for yourself to, you're basically asking for your marriage not to work because in order for it to work, you must work.
